\end{center}

\noindent Randomized-smoothing certified accuracy vs.\ $L_2$ radius on the verified
nets (Cohen et al.\ 2019; $n{=}10112$ samples, $\alpha{=}0.001$; forward-only). Each
curve is one noise level $\sigma$; the vertical drop is the $n$-ceiling
$\sigma\,\Phi^{-1}(\alpha^{1/n})$. The Lipschitz-product certificate is $0\%$ at every
radius on these same nets. Average certified radius (ACR) per $\sigma$:
MNIST-MLP 0.36/0.70/1.13; MNIST-CNN 0.38/0.75/1.28; CIFAR-CNN 0.27/0.40.
